heading: Repealed. , title XIII, § 1313,July 1, 1944, ch. 37358 Stat. 714
label: 6 to 15a Repealed. , title XIII, § 1313,July 1, 1944, ch. 37358 Stat. 714
notes: Section 6, acts , ; , , provided for care of sick and disabled seamen. See .July 1, 1902, ch. 1370, § 132 Stat. 712Aug. 14, 1912, ch. 288, § 137 Stat. 309section 249 of this title

Section 6a, , , provided for care of seamen on Government vessels not in Military or Naval Establishments and of cadets on State school ships. See .act Mar. 31, 1936, ch. 16149 Stat. 1185section 249 of this title
Section 7, , , provided for investigations by Service into diseases, etc., and publications relating thereto. See .act Aug. 14, 1912, ch. 288, § 137 Stat. 309section 241 of this title
Section 8, acts , ; , , provided for use of Service in time of war. See .July 1, 1902, ch. 1370, § 432 Stat. 713Aug. 14, 1912, ch. 288, § 137 Stat. 309section 217 of this title
Section 8a, , , provided for extension of facilities of Service to health officials and scientists. See .act Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 2(a)46 Stat. 150section 241 of this title
Section 9, , , provided for suppression of Spanish influenza and other communicable diseases. See .act Oct. 1, 1918, ch. 179, § 240 Stat. 1008section 264 of this title

Section 9a, , , provided that administrative office and bureau divisions in District of Columbia be a part of departmental organization and scientific offices and research laboratories as part of field services. See .act Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 346 Stat. 150section 203 of this title

Section 10, acts , ; , ; , , provided for appointment of Surgeon General. See .Mar. 3, 1875, ch. 13018 Stat. 377July 1, 1902, ch. 1370, § 132 Stat. 712Aug. 14, 1912, ch. 288, § 137 Stat. 309section 205 of this title
Section 11, R.S. § 4802; acts , ; , , provided for supervisory powers of Surgeon General. See .July 1, 1902, ch. 1370, § 132 Stat. 712Aug. 14, 1912, ch. 288, § 137 Stat. 309section 203 of this title

Section 11a, , , provided for pay and allowances of Surgeon General and for reversion in grade on expiration of term. See sections 205 and 207 of this title.act Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 10(b)46 Stat. 152
Section 11b, , , provided for rank and pay of Assistant to Surgeon General. See sections 206 and 207 of this title.act Aug. 9, 1939, ch. 60653 Stat. 1266

Section 12, acts , ; , ; , , provided for appointment of medical officers after examination. See .Jan. 4, 1889, ch. 19, § 125 Stat. 639July 1, 1902, ch. 1370, § 132 Stat. 712Aug. 14, 1912, ch. 288, § 137 Stat. 309section 209 of this title
Section 13, , , provided for original appointments as assistant surgeons and promotion of passed assistant surgeon. See sections 209 and 211 of this title.act Jan. 4, 1889, ch. 19, § 225 Stat. 639
Section 14, , , provided for help as provided by Congress. See .act Aug. 14, 1912, ch. 288, § 237 Stat. 309section 209 of this title

Section 15, acts , ; , ; , , provided for details for duty in bureau. See .Mar. 3, 1891, ch. 54126 Stat. 923July 31, 1894, ch. 17428 Stat. 179July 1, 1902, ch. 1370, § 132 Stat. 712section 215 of this title

Section 15a, acts , ; , ; , , provided for detail of two hospital attendants for duty in laboratory. See .Mar. 2, 1895, ch. 177, § 128 Stat. 780July 1, 1902, ch. 1370, § 132 Stat. 712Aug. 14, 1912, ch. 288, § 137 Stat. 309section 215 of this title
